tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, indicating possible nephropathy and metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c is unusual and may reflect anemia or recent changes in glucose control. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has abnormal odor, but no evidence of infection. Low CO₂ may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. Recommend further renal assessment and clinical correlation for possible diabetic nephropathy or other renal pathology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
